query_log.query: 2026-03-19 Reuters U.S. stocks close FOMC Treasury yields dollar yen oil copper Japan stocks
2026-03-19 シカゴ日経225先物 TOPIX先物 53,465 大阪日中比 1,445円安
2026-03-18 TDnet 業績予想 修正 配当 自己株買い みんかぶ
2026-03-19 Reuters Japan stocks outlook FOMC BOJ Micron oil

snapshot: U.S. equities sold off sharply after the FOMC. The Dow fell 768 points, the U.S. 10-year yield rose to 4.261%, and USD/JPY traded around 159.7.
WTI settled at $96.32, but after-hours trade moved back toward $100 following the Qatar energy-facility attack report. Oil risk is still alive.
Chicago Nikkei 225 futures were 53,465, down 1,445 versus Osaka day session. Reuters' morning range for the Nikkei was 53,200 to 54,300.
The Nikkei opened at 54,287.80 and by 10:00 JST was at 53,762.84, down 1,476.56, with 1,499 decliners across the market.
With the BOJ decision, Governor Ueda's press conference, and the Japan-U.S. summit ahead, the tape favors relative-strength names backed by buybacks, dividend hikes and revisions.

drivers.driver: Post-FOMC U.S. equity shock, plus renewed oil and FX pressure
drivers.why_today: After the FOMC, U.S. equities dropped hard, the U.S. 10-year rose to 4.261%, USD/JPY pushed toward 159.90, and crude moved back toward $100. That is a clear headwind for the broad index.

drivers.driver: Buybacks, dividend hikes and guidance revisions that still work in a weak tape
drivers.why_today: BayCurrent, TOA, Tokyo Kikai and Meiho Facility all posted clear after-close catalysts, and capital is concentrating in the few names still showing relative strength.

headline_inventory.headline: Reuters' New York market summary showed a 768-point Dow drop, USD/JPY around 159.7, the U.S. 10-year at 4.261%, and persistent oil risk
Chicago Nikkei 225 futures were 53,465, down 1,445 versus Osaka day session, while USD/JPY was 159.70-80
USD/JPY was seen in a 159.00-160.50 range, with both post-BOJ volatility risk and intervention concern in view
The Nikkei opened down at 54,287.80 and quickly slipped into the 53,800 area
At 10:00 JST the Nikkei was 53,762.84, down 1,476.56, with 1,499 losers versus only 63 gainers
BayCurrent disclosed a buyback capped at 4.3% of shares outstanding and the full cancellation of acquired shares
TOA raised both full-year guidance and its year-end dividend outlook
Tokyo Kikai raised its FY March 2026 net-profit outlook on litigation-related income and related items
Meiho Facility raised its FY March 2026 year-end dividend forecast by 0.5 yen
Micron's third-quarter sales outlook beat expectations, but the stock still fell about 4% after hours, leaving mixed read-through for semis

watchlist.name: BayCurrent
watchlist.why_today: It disclosed a buyback capped at 4.3% plus full cancellation of acquired shares. Even in a washout tape, it is clearly standing out near the top of Prime gainers.
watchpoints: Can it stay near the top of Prime gainers despite the market-wide selloff
Can it build around the 4,700 yen area with real turnover
Does buying remain even ahead of the BOJ and FX event risk
falsifiers: Broader risk-off pressure erases the relative-strength profile
The stock breaks back below the 4,600 yen area after the first buyback-driven burst
watchlist.red_team: The repurchase does not start until mid-April, so today's move is partly expectation-led rather than immediate cash demand.

watchlist.name: TOA
watchlist.why_today: It raised full-year guidance and the year-end dividend after the close. The fact that it is still higher in a weak market gives the numbers more weight.
watchpoints: Can it hold the 1,800 yen area
Does the market reward the higher net profit and dividend more than the flat operating-profit line
Can the weaker-yen angle keep attracting incremental buyers
falsifiers: Investors judge the revision to be too dependent on below-the-line items
The move fades quickly on thin follow-through volume
watchlist.red_team: Tax credits and received-dividend gains contributed meaningfully, so the market may debate the quality of the upgrade.

watchlist.name: Tokyo Kikai Seisakusho
watchlist.why_today: Litigation-related income drove a sharp raise to the net-profit outlook and the stock briefly hit limit-up territory. Even if it is mostly a price-range trade, it is hard to ignore.
watchpoints: Can it absorb selling even after approaching limit-up levels
Does the market focus on the net-profit impact rather than the one-off nature of the gain
Can it move into a high-level consolidation despite the thin order book
falsifiers: The one-off profit nature becomes the dominant narrative
The stock breaks below the early spike high after the sharp opening move
watchlist.red_team: Because the catalyst is centered on special profit, repeatability is weak and the move can turn into pure volatility.

watchlist.name: INPEX
watchlist.why_today: Crude moved back toward $100 and USD/JPY stayed in the upper 159s. In a falling market, energy remains one of the cleaner relative hedges to monitor.
watchpoints: Does the market continue to price the move back toward $100 oil during Tokyo hours
Can the stock hold relative strength against a sharply weaker Nikkei
Does USD/JPY stay in the upper 159 range
falsifiers: A stronger yen removes the external macro tailwind
watchlist.red_team: Even crude beneficiaries can be sold in an indiscriminate market-wide de-risking phase.

watchlist.name: Meiho Facility Works
watchlist.why_today: It raised the year-end dividend by 0.5 yen. The catalyst is modest, but it is useful for checking whether dividend names can still stay resilient in a weak market.
watchpoints: Can it hold the 1,060 yen area
Does money keep rotating into smaller dividend names
falsifiers: The market judges the dividend increase to be too small
Worsening market tone pulls capital out of smaller stocks
watchlist.red_team: The catalyst is straightforward but small, so the depth of follow-through may be limited.

deep_dive.title: BayCurrent: In a down-1,400-point tape, test whether shareholder-return strength can still lead

lines: When more than 90% of Prime names are falling, supply-improvement catalysts can matter more than ordinary earnings narratives.
A buyback capped at 4.3% plus full cancellation of acquired shares gives BayCurrent a cleaner relative-strength case than most names in today's tape.
After the March 18 close, the company disclosed the buyback and the August cancellation plan, and Reuters confirmed it was still among the top Prime gainers on March 19 morning.
Even at 10:00 JST, it remained one of the few positive contributors in an otherwise broad selloff, which makes the catalyst easier to validate intraday.
Can it hold around the 4,700 yen area instead of fading after the opening burst.
Even if the broader market stays weak ahead of BOJ and FX events, does dip-buying continue with real volume.
Broader risk-off selling intensifies enough to wipe out the relative-strength profile.
A drop back below the 4,600 yen area makes the post-disclosure fade unmistakable.
The repurchase starts on April 15, so today's move is partly expectation-led and vulnerable to a fade.
If the post-FOMC U.S. growth-stock weakness spills over, consultants and IT-services names can still get dragged lower.

no_trade_conditions: If the BOJ decision or Governor Ueda's press conference causes a sharp USD/JPY reversal and completely changes the market's leadership
If the Nikkei selloff deepens further and even buyback or dividend-hike names lose their relative strength
If BayCurrent or TOA quickly break their opening highs and short-term money clearly exits
If crude reverses sharply lower and the macro-hedge premise for INPEX breaks
